LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA INTEGRATED WASTE MANAGEMENT BOARD DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO FIRST, SECOND AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION PETITIONERS FAILED TO PLEAD COMPLIANCE WITH THE TORT CLAIMS ACT. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PETITIONER MUST PLEAD THE NATURE OF THE TAKING. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM THAT IS PROPERLY SUBJECT TO A MOTION TO STRIKE, NOT A DEMURRER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Respondent California Integrated Waste Management Board demurrer to first amended petition for writ of mandate and complaint for inverse condemnation, unfair competition, and related relief. Appearances: Charles W. Getz, IV, Esq. Deputy Attorney General, (415) 703-5501 appearing for Defendants/Respondents and moving party; William Siamas, Esq. of Morgan Franich Fredkin & Marsh (408) 288-8288 appearing for Petitioners/Plaintiffs and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled, 10 days leave to answer. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant California Integrated Waste Management Board motion to strike first amended complaint. Appearances: Charles W. Getz, IV, Esq. Deputy Attorney General, (415) 703-5501 appearing for Defendants/Respondents and moving party; William Siamas, Esq., Morgan Franich Fredkin & Marsh (408) 288-8288 appearing for Petitioners/Plaintiffs and opposing party. (This tentative ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. Plaintiffs may seek leave to amend to allege punitive damages when appropriate. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA INTEGRATED WASTE MANAGEMENT BOARD DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE and COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part, with ten days leave to amend. As to first, second and fifth causes of action petitioners failed to plead compliance with the Tort Claims Act. As to the fourth cause of action, petitioner must plead the nature of the taking. Demurrer overruled as to punitive damages claim that is properly subject to a motion to strike, not a demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
